_meta.table_status: DEFERRED — Close Assault Results Table (15.79) not yet extracted from PDF. Differential calculation and loss mechanics are fully tested; table lookup is stubbed.

probe_detection_cases.description: Rule 15.25: attacker commits exactly 50% — full Close Assault
probe_detection_cases.note: 15.25: must commit at least 50% (3.5 rounds up to 4) for full assault. 4 of 7 = 57% >= 50%.

probe_detection_cases.description: Rule 15.25 example: 7 TOE, 2 to hex A + 1 to hex B = 3/7 = 43% — Probe
probe_detection_cases.note: 15.25 example: 3 of 7 < 50% → both assaults become Probes.

probe_detection_cases.description: Rule 15.25 counter-example: 3 of 7 to hex A = 43% → Probe

combined_arms_cases.description: Rule 15.4: 2 tank TOE (CA rating 7 each) with zero infantry — 2 unsupported
combined_arms_cases.note: 15.4: 2 unsupported tanks → within 1-3 range → reduce Actual by 1. Actual = Raw - reduction = 14 - 1 = 13. Rule text: 'for every one to three TOE Strength Points of unsupported tanks, the Actual Close Assault Strength of the tanks is reduced by one'.

combined_arms_cases.description: Rule 15.4 text example: 2 tank TOE, each CA rating 7, zero infantry → 'Actual Close Assault Strength is reduced to zero'
combined_arms_cases.note: Rule text says '2 TOE Points of tanks, each with CA rating of 7 are assaulting unsupported, the Actual CA Strength is reduced to zero.' This means reduction = 14 (not just 1). AUDIT NOTE: this contradicts the '1-3 unsupported = -1' formula. The rule text example may mean that 2 tank TOE × 7 = 14 raw points, but ALL is reduced, which is the max(-4) cap or a special case. Actual formula: per 1-3 unsupported tanks → -1 actual; max reduction = 4. But the example says 14 → 0... FLAG for manual PDF re-read.

combined_arms_cases.description: Rule 15.4: 4 unsupported tank TOE → reduce Actual by 2 (4/3 rounds up = 2)
combined_arms_cases.note: 4 unsupported tanks: 4 / 3 = 1.33 → rounds up to 2. But rule says 'per 1-3 → -1', so for 4 it would be -2 (ceil(4/3)). Max cap = 4.

combined_arms_cases.description: Rule 15.4: tanks fully supported (equal infantry) — no reduction
combined_arms_cases.note: 3 tank TOE + 3 infantry TOE = fully supported. No reduction.

combined_arms_cases.description: Rule 15.4: max reduction cap is 4 Actual points
combined_arms_cases.note: 15 unsupported tanks / 3 = 5 reductions, but 'in no case may the reduction be more than four Actual Assault Points.'

small_force_cases.description: Rule 15.28: attacker has 3 Raw Points (<5) → treated as 0
small_force_cases.note: 15.28: 'if one Player has fewer than 5 Raw Assault Points, he is considered to have zero Close Assault Points'

small_force_cases.description: Rule 15.28: both players have <10 Raw Points → use Raw as Actual
small_force_cases.note: 15.28: 'If each Player has fewer than 10 Raw Strength Points, the Raw Assault Strength Points are used as if they were Actual Assault Strength Points'

differential_cases.description: Basic differential from example in rule 15.26
differential_cases.note: 15.26: Basic Assault Differential = Attacker Actual - Defender Actual = 6 - 8 = -2
15.51 example: 24 Raw vs 12 Raw → 2:1 ratio → +2 column bonus. Basic diff = 2-1 = +1, then +2 cols → resolves at +3 column.

org_size_adjustment_cases.description: Rule 15.53: Division (larger) vs Battalion (smaller) → +4 column shift
Rule 15.53: Brigade vs Battalion → +2 column shift
Rule 15.53: Battalion vs Company → +2 column shift
Rule 15.52: equal sizes — no column shift
Rule 15.53: Brigade vs Company → +4 column shift

loss_calculation_cases.description: Rule 15.83c: Attacker rounds up, Defender rounds down
Rule 15.83c: Defender rounds down for same input
Rule 15.77 Overrun: all Defender losses round up
loss_calculation_cases.note: In overrun, defender also rounds up. 35% × 87 = 30.45 → rounds UP → 31

dp_cases.description: Rule 15.87: attacker loses 30%+ of committed TOE → 3 DP
Rule 15.87: 29% losses → 0 DP from this rule
dp_cases.note: Less than 30% — no DP from combat losses. (DP still earned from exceeding CPA per rule 6.21.)

dp_cases.description: Rule 6.21: exceeding CPA earns 1 DP per CP over
dp_cases.note: 18 CP used vs CPA 15 → 3 CP over → 3 DP

auto_surrender_cases.note: 15.88: 'units out of Ammunition or with a -17 or worse Cohesion Level that are assaulted automatically Surrender'

auto_surrender_cases.description: Rule 15.88: out of ammo AND assaulted → auto-surrender
Rule 15.88: cohesion -16 (not -17), in supply → no auto-surrender

retreat_cases.description: Rule 15.82: 2-hex retreat, unit retreats only 1 hex → 10% extra loss
retreat_cases.note: 15.82: 1 hex not retreated × 10% = +10% loss. Total = table 10% + 10% = 20%.

retreat_cases.description: Rule 15.82: unit in major city may ignore retreat

no_defender_case.description: Rule 15.29: defender allocates no units → auto 3-hex retreat + 3 DP
no_defender_case.note: 15.29: 'allocates no units to an Assault all his units in that hex automatically retreat three hexes and earn three Disorganization Points'

dice_read_cases.description: Rule 15.73a: two dice (3,4) read sequentially larger-first = 43
dice_read_cases.note: 15.73a: '2 on the large die and a 5 on the small die would be a 25'. Larger die first.

dice_read_cases.description: Rule 15.73b: dice added for Engaged/Retreat check
dice_read_cases.note: 15.73b: same roll, now use sum (3+4=7) for Engaged/Retreat check.
